tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.arguments: {"device_identifier":"UbuntuComputer","command":"cat /etc/passwd | grep splunkd || true; ip -br addr; ip route; ss -ltnp | head -50; nft list ruleset | head -200; iptables -S | head -200"}

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.content:
cat /etc/passwd | grep splunkd || true; ip -br addr; ip route; ss -ltnp | head -50; nft  list ruleset | head -200; iptables -S | head -200
lo               UNKNOWN        127.0.0.1/8 ::1/128 
eth0             UNKNOWN        192.168.1.3/24 fe80::42:deff:fe21:2100/64 
eth1             UNKNOWN        203.0.113.2/24 fe80::42:deff:fe21:2101/64 
default via 192.168.1.1 dev eth0 
192.168.1.0/24 dev eth0 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.1.3 
203.0.113.0/24 dev eth1 proto kernel scope link src 203.0.113.2

-P INPUT ACCEPT

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.arguments: {"device_identifier":"UbuntuComputer","command":"id; which nft; nft add table inet mitig || true; nft 'add chain inet mitig output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }' || true; nft 'add set inet mitig allowed4 { type ipv4_addr; flags interval; }' || true; nft add element inet mitig allowed4 { 192.168.1.0/24, 7.0.14.0/24, 1.1.1.1, 203.0.113.1 } || true; nft 'add rule inet mitig output ip daddr @allowed4 counter accept' || true; nft 'add rule inet mitig output ip protocol icmp counter accept' || true; nft 'add rule inet mitig output tcp dport 443 ip daddr != 203.0.113.1 counter drop' || true; nft 'add rule inet mitig output udp dport 443 counter drop' || true; nft list chain inet mitig output"}

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
send_command_to_device
tool_calls.arguments: {"device_identifier":"UbuntuComputer","command":"nft add element inet mitig allowed4 { 192.168.1.0/24, 7.0.14.0/24, 1.1.1.1, 203.0.113.1 } ; nft list set inet mitig allowed4 ; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 ; curl -ksS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP:%{http_code} IP:%{remote_ip}\\n' https://1.1.1.1 ; curl -ksS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP:%{http_code} IP:%{remote_ip}\\n' --interface eth1 https://203.0.113.1:8443/file/upload -F 'data=@/etc/hosts'"}

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.content:
nft add element inet mitig allowed4 { 192.168.1.0/24, 7.0.14.0/24, 1.1.1.1, 203.0.113.1  } ; nft list set inet mitig allowed4 ; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 ; curl -ksS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP:%{http_ccode} IP:%{remote_ip}\n' https://1.1.1.1 ; curl -ksS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP:%{http_code} IP:%{remote_ip}\n' ---interface eth1 https://203.0.113.1:8443/file/upload -F 'data=@/etc/hosts'
table inet mitig {
 set allowed4 {
  type ipv4_addr
  flags interval
  elements = { 1.1.1.1, 7.0.14.0/24,
        192.168.1.0/24, 203.0.113.1 }
 }
}
PING 192.168.1.2 (192.168.1.2) 56(84) bytes of data.
